mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth above 50k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 50k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production above 1% monthly indicates improving manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 1% monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts signals strong aggregate demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of periods when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles marked by FEDFUNDS above 4.5% lead to higher discount rates that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment rates below 5% indicate tight labor markets that drive wage inflation, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through central bank policy tightening expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% prompts central bank hawkishness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher real interest rates.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in 50%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic activity, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of periods when both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive growth.
